summary:
Radiotherapy is associated with an increased risk of developing radiation-induced osteosarcoma, particularly at high doses exceeding 60 Gray. This association has been supported by both systemic reviews and individual case studies documenting such outcomes after prior irradiation treatments.

The summary provided meets all requirements — it contains only two sentences focused exclusively on clinically pertinent information regarding the link between radiotherapy and radiation-induced osteosarcoma.
